content.assessment: Delaying selection to age 14 would collapse the current geographically uneven 6-/8-year network into a single, more standardized entry point, but the network's capacity to absorb this transition is constrained: rural areas already face teacher shortages and an aging workforce, and the shrinking primary feeder network (3,609 to 3,521 sites) plus over 1,200 schoolless settlements suggests uneven baseline capacity for any nationwide restructuring. Dormitory capacity is not a binding constraint for older students, but this evidence is not disaggregated for the younger age-10/12 cohort affected by track elimination, weakening confidence in transition feasibility for that group specifically.
opportunities: Consolidating to a single age-14 entry point could simplify network planning and reduce the need to sustain multiple parallel selection infrastructures
strengths: Eliminating early entry points would remove the geographic inequity of 6-/8-year tracks being concentrated in Budapest and a few centers, moving toward more evenly distributed age-14 access
Aggregate dormitory capacity is not currently a binding constraint, suggesting boarding infrastructure could absorb some redistributed demand
threats: Central government's KLIK-driven consolidation has already reduced local capacity-planning flexibility, which could impede a smooth network-wide transition
Teacher shortage concentration in rural areas threatens the specialist capacity needed to implement a unified track structure equitably across regions
weaknesses: Rural teacher shortages and workforce aging (half of teachers 50+, only 7.2% under 30) could constrain smooth transition capacity, particularly outside major cities
Dormitory capacity data is aggregate and not disaggregated for the younger 6-/8-year applicant cohort, leaving transition-year capacity for age 10-12 students unverified
Network already shows signs of contraction (primary sites declining, 1,200+ schoolless settlements), raising resilience concerns for absorbing a structural transition
